title: Orchestrator Prompt — Phase 2.5: Product Acceptance
what_it_is: The operating prompt for a fresh orchestrator session running Phase 2.5 (Product Acceptance E2E) of the Orvex Studio delivery program — proving the assembled product works via real Playwright/Clerk journeys against the real deployed family, ending in a human PO acceptance sitting. It explicitly inherits the operating contract, doctrine, and Act-3 delivery-loop mechanics from the Delivery Orchestrator prompt (gkkUDzn277) and specializes that loop for product acceptance rather than restating it; its entry criteria gate on Phase-2 exit being green per the program plan (5eFdxN3edd). It runs 5 stages (harness/Clerk stand-up, parity-matrix build, tiered R1-R14 execution, defect triage, human acceptance sitting) and hands off explicitly to a not-yet-authored Phase-3 orchestrator prompt.
overlaps: None causing confusion. It is one of the three current phase orchestrator prompts (P1 yXUWpQpRjx, P2 Ng66su4dVG, P2.5 ErYdXzIj6g) named in the current 4-phase Delivery Program (5eFdxN3edd), and cites the program plan, brief (rgBOQh31p3), Coding Standards (6aMAzsYeQb), SE-Arch (8sYi523i4t), Issue Authoring (9VUHxAcoXw), relevant ADRs, and the Houston E2E pattern as canon inputs rather than duplicating them. It does not overlap with the new parallel-build-plan.md (8-teams-by-service, contract-tag unlock): that plan concerns Phase-2 'Isolated Builds' execution structure, while this page's ENTRY CRITERIA explicitly presume Phase-2 is already exited/green before Phase 2.5 (acceptance testing) begins — they are sequential, non-overlapping phases. The parallel-build-plan should be folded into the Phase-2 prompt (Ng66su4dVG) or as a child of the program plan (5eFdxN3edd), not here.
